bug.title: Checkpoint-file resume disables git checkpointing (behavioral regression)
bug.description: When using `fabro resume --checkpoint <path> --workflow <path>`, the RunConfig is constructed with `git_checkpoint_enabled: false`, `run_branch: None`, `meta_branch: None`, and `host_repo_path: None`. The resumed run will NOT create git checkpoints after each stage, so if it fails partway through the user cannot resume again. The old `fabro run --resume` flow configured a worktree and enabled git checkpointing, so chained resumes worked. The branch-resume path (`fabro resume <run-id>`) is unaffected.

bug.title: Devcontainer Dockerfile not bridged to Daytona config on resume — wrong sandbox image
bug.description: In `run.rs`, when devcontainer is enabled and the sandbox is Daytona, `devcontainer_bridge::devcontainer_to_snapshot_config()` converts the devcontainer Dockerfile into a Daytona snapshot config. In `resume.rs`, both prepare paths resolve the Daytona config with `resolve_daytona_config(None, run_defaults).unwrap_or_default()` — the devcontainer Dockerfile is never bridged. Resuming a Daytona + devcontainer run will spin up a sandbox with the default image instead of the custom devcontainer image.

bug.title: Missing @file reference resolution in goal override
bug.description: In `run.rs`, after `apply_goal_override`, `resolve_file_ref()` expands `@file` references in the goal text. In `resume.rs`, both `prepare_from_checkpoint` and `prepare_from_branch` call `apply_goal_override` but never resolve `@file` references. Passing `--goal '@goals/plan.md'` to `fabro resume` will use the literal string instead of the file contents.

bug.title: Progress UI finished before retro, suppressing retro progress display
bug.description: `progress_ui.finish()` is called at line 1025 before the retro runs at line 1127. In `run.rs`, the comment says "Finish progress bars after retro (retro stage uses the same ProgressUI)" and `finish()` is called AFTER `generate_retro()`. Since the retro agent emits events handled by ProgressUI, finishing early means the user sees no progress indication during a multi-second LLM call — the CLI appears to hang.

bug.title: Missing sandbox cleanup guard — leaked remote resources on panic
bug.description: In `run.rs`, a `cleanup_guard` ensures the sandbox is cleaned up if the function panics after sandbox creation. `resume.rs` has no equivalent guard. If `run_resumed()` panics between sandbox creation and the explicit `engine.cleanup_sandbox()` call at line 1259, remote sandboxes (Daytona VMs, SSH sessions, Exe instances) will be leaked indefinitely.

bug.title: No backwards-compatible alias or helpful error for removed --resume and --run-branch flags
bug.description: The `--resume` and `--run-branch` flags on `fabro run` were removed outright. User scripts, CI pipelines, or documentation using these flags will get a generic clap "unexpected argument" error with no guidance to use `fabro resume` instead.

bug.title: Missing devcontainer initializeCommand execution on checkpoint-file resume
bug.description: In `run.rs`, devcontainer `initialize_commands` are executed on the host before sandbox creation. In `resume.rs`, neither prepare path executes them. For branch-resume this is arguably fine (they ran originally), but for checkpoint-resume creating a fresh sandbox, missing these host-side prep commands (certificates, mount directories, etc.) can cause setup failures.

bug.title: Status guard writes misleading SandboxInitFailed for engine panics
bug.description: The `status_guard` writes `SandboxInitFailed` on drop. In `run.rs` it is defused before `engine.run()` so it only fires for actual sandbox init failures. In `resume.rs` it spans the entire `engine.run_with_lifecycle()` and is defused after (line 1022). If the engine panics during workflow execution, `fabro ps` will show "SandboxInitFailed" instead of the actual failure reason.
